selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw:
{
"mode": "llm",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's crucial to have a comprehensive strategy in place. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Identify the source of the fake content: Is it coming from a competitor, a disgruntled employee, or someone else? Understanding the source will help you tailor your response accordingly. 2) Document evidence: Collect screenshots, URLs, and any other relevant proof to support your claims. This will come in handy when dealing with social media platforms or law enforcement agencies. 3) Report fake content: Most social media platforms have a reporting mechanism in place. Use this feature to flag the fake content for removal. 4) Communicate directly with the creator of the fake content: If you can identify the person responsible for creating and sharing false information, consider reaching out to them directly. Politely explain how their actions are harmful and ask them to retract or correct the fake content. 5) Educate your followers: Share tips on how to spot fake content and encourage them to fact-check information before sharing it. By empowering your followers, you can create a culture of critical thinking and media literacy within your online community. Finally, stay up-to-date with the latest social media trends and best practices for managing fake content. By following these steps, you'll be well on your way to effectively dealing with fake content on social media.",
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to have a comprehensive strategy in place. This includes identifying the source of the fake content, documenting evidence, reporting false content, communicating directly with the creator, educating followers, and staying up-to-date with the latest trends and best practices.",
"tone": "informative",
"citations": [],

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it's crucial to have a comprehensive strategy in place. Here are some steps you can take: 1) Identify the source of the fake content: Is it coming from a competitor, a disgruntled employee, or someone else? Understanding the source will help you tailor your response accordingly. 2) Document evidence: Collect screenshots, URLs, and any other relevant proof to support your claims. This will come in handy when dealing with social media platforms or law enforcement agencies. 3) Report fake content: Most social media platforms have a reporting mechanism in place. Use this feature to flag the fake content for removal. 4) Communicate directly with the creator of the fake content: If you can identify the person responsible for creating and sharing false information, consider reaching out to them directly. Politely explain how their actions are harmful and ask them to retract or correct the fake content. 5) Educate your followers: Share tips on how to spot fake content and encourage them to fact-check information before sharing it. By empowering your followers, you can create a culture of critical thinking and media literacy within your online community. Finally, stay up-to-date with the latest social media trends and best practices for managing fake content. By following these steps, you'll be well on your way to effectively dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to have a comprehensive strategy in place. This includes identifying the source of the fake content, documenting evidence, reporting false content, communicating directly with the creator, educating followers, and staying up-to-date with the latest trends and best practices.
